text:
Orarge (オラルジ, Oraruji) is a member of the Heil-Ly Family. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 378 

Appearance

Orarge has distinctive black marks below his eyes and at the corners of his mouth and distinctive eyebrows. He has long hair tied in a ponytail and wears a bandana with an intricate design. He is seen wearing a black shirt with a patterned vest and striped pants. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 399 On the twelfth day, he is seen wearing a baggy satin dress shirt with a striped collar and black khaki pants. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 407 

Personality

Orarge appears to be an obedient Heil-Ly member. He made sure to ask Yokotani a question regarding the law and chastised Perigord for forgetting Morena's instructions. He is important to Morena as he is seen standing around the game table durning the card game. Like his fellow Heil-Ly members, he seems to have sadistic tendencies and no qualms about killing people.

Plot

Succession Contest arc

On the fourth day of the voyage, Orarge is first seen with the other Heil-Ly members as Morena infects them with Contagion one by one before explaining how the point system of her Nen ability would work. 

On the tenth day of the voyage, The date this occurs is ambiguous and could be anywhere from the afternoon of the tenth day to the end of the eleventh day Orarge is one of those who congratulate Bille as he reaches level 21. 

Later that day, Morena meets up with several of her underlings in the Heil-Ly Family secret hideout and learns that they had been unable to reach a butler in the VVIP area, their liaison with 4 th Prince Tserriednich. She guesses that Tserriednich might have realized what they are up to and informs the members meeting her that they were running out of time until their place was discovered, evidenced by Gateaume's report. 

Morena tells them that she wants someone to capture one of Tserriednich's personal soldiers to "recruit" while helping Dogman. Matvere says he would prefer to be the "organ", so Sodom volunteers to go. Morena predicts that Tserriednich will want to deal with her, the traitor, himself, and tells them that she wants to infect someone on his side with Contagion to track his movements. She reminds them that they cannot use Room 3101 anymore and to enter from Door C. Morena turns to Orarge and Yokotani and instructs them to continue to keep watch over the door, and to expect only military and yakuza from then on. Orarge remarks that 1 st Prince Benjamin's men turning up would be bad, and asks Yokotani if there is any lawful basis on which to send them away. Yokotani, a lawyer, smiles and says that there are none. After they leave, Morena continues giving instructions to other Heil-Ly members. 

On the twelfth day of the voyage, Hinrigh and Nobunaga infiltrate the Heil-Ly's secret hideout. Orarge reads a copy of Weekly Shōnen Jump, ignoring Hinrigh and Nobunaga. To get their attention, Hinrigh begins throwing knives at them, and one of them heads straight towards Perigord, seated on Orarge's right. Perigord sticks his hand out, wondering how long it would take for his hand to heal if the knife went through it, but Orarge stops the knife with his reading material before chastising Perigord for not evading and asking him to remember what he was using his ability for. Orarge reminds him that he had already been chosen as the "organ" and that Morena told him to keep his head down, earning an apology from Perigord. 

Later that day, Morena informs her underlings that the funeral procession 9 th Prince Halkenburg would be taking place, and that the large crowd gathering for the send-off is a perfect opportunity for them. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 405 The Heil-Ly capture PFC Borksen on Tier 3 while she is on patrol during the funeral procession, as the funeral procession passes by her. She is brought to the Heil-Ly Family Secret Hideout, where she regains consciousness only to find herself surrounded by Morena and five of her underlings, including Orarge. Orarge stands behind Borksen on her left with his thumbs in his pockets, watching Borksen as Morena explains the rules of a negotiation game to Borksen. 

Orarge assumes responsibility for shuffling Borksen's cards during the game, using a Hindu shuffle whenever the time comes for Borksen to pick a card to discard. Orarge shuffles the cards and lays them neatly in front of Borksen before she picks her first and second card to discard, but not before she picks her third card to discard. During Morena's explanation for the "Ability" card, she threatens Borksen by saying that she is replaceable. Orarge watches Borksen for a reaction, but Borksen does not react. The speaker beeps and interrupts the game to announce that Special Martial Law has been declared. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 408 Orarge listens to it with the others, with his hands in his pockets. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 409 

Despite instructions being given for each tier of the Black Whale, Borksen continues the game. She chooses the "Deal" card and agrees to kiss Morena for the chance to return a card from the graveyard, even though Morena explains that it is one of the three conditions needed to join the Heil-Ly. 

After Borksen takes the "X" card back, Orarge reaches over to take her three cards and shuffle them, but Borksen tells him to wait. She asks to check the two facedown cards, saying that she took her eyes off them when Morena was kissing her. After Borksen verifies the cards, Orarge shuffles them and lays them in front of Borksen to pick. The "X" card that Borksen had just returned to her hand is removed, and the game continues. 

Orarge continues spectating, and shuffles Borksen's final two cards before watching intently to see whether Borksen would end with a "Return", which she had earlier stated would be used to return the "X" card and void the game, or a "No". Borksen flips over the "Return" card, and in her relief at surviving, grabs it tightly enough to crumple. Orarge and the other underlings leave, not believing they need to watch Borksen return the "X" card, but hear Morena ask Borksen if she had made a mistake, and turn to see that Borksen has chosen to return the "Yes" card. Morena's tone changes, and she accuses Borksen of cheating, by marking the "Return" card in a way that neither she nor Orarge could see. Orarge and the other underlings return and surround her. Morena reveals that cheating in the card game triggers a manipulative ability that forces its targets to say only Yes or No, and hears Borksen affirm one more time that she will join the Heil-Ly. Orarge and the other underlings applaud her choice, and Borksen is released from the manipulative ability, collapsing to the ground. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 410 

Abilities & Powers

Advanced Speed and Reflexes: Orarge has quick reflexes, being able to react to a knife being thrown at an ally and intercept it with a book he was holding. 

Nen

Orarge is an Enhancer. Having reached level 21 with Morena's Nen ability, Orarge's aura amount and output have been increased. He also obtained a Nen ability, which remains unknown. 

Manga Appearances

Battles

Trivia

Intertextuality and References

Orarge's general appearance is most likely based on Gama from YuYu Hakusho, a series also written and illustrated by Yoshihiro Togashi. In addition to wearing a bandana with an intricate design, the character eventually puts dark paint on his body, including around his eyes and mouth. YuYu Hakusho - Volume 8, Chapter 67
